returns.description: 1-bar percentage change in close price; primary short-term momentum signal

log_returns.description: Natural log of close/prev_close; better than pct_change for modelling compounding and handles large swings more symmetrically

hl_range.description: (high - low) / close; intraday range normalised by price — high values indicate volatility or indecision, low values indicate calm trending bars

oc_range.description: (close - open) / open; candle body direction and size — positive = bullish bar, negative = bearish bar, magnitude = conviction

ema_ratio_price_20.description: close / EMA(20); how stretched price is above or below its 20-bar mean — above 1.0 = overextended bullish, below 1.0 = oversold or bearish

ema_ratio_20_50.description: EMA(20) / EMA(50); short-term vs medium-term trend alignment — above 1.0 means short trend is bullish relative to medium trend

ema_ratio_50_200.description: EMA(50) / EMA(200); medium vs long-term trend — golden cross when crosses above 1.0, death cross when below; the slowest-moving trend signal

macd_hist_norm.description: MACD histogram / close; momentum acceleration normalised by price — positive and rising = strengthening uptrend, negative and falling = strengthening downtrend

macd_signal_norm.description: MACD signal line / close; smoothed momentum baseline — histogram crossing above signal is the classic buy trigger

rsi.description: RSI(14) in [0, 100]; mean-reversion oscillator — below 30 = oversold (potential buy), above 70 = overbought (potential sell), 50 = neutral

stoch_k.description: Stochastic %K(14,3); compares close to the 14-bar high/low range — fast momentum oscillator similar to RSI but more sensitive to recent extremes

stoch_d.description: Stochastic %D; 3-bar SMA of %K — slower line used for crossover signals: %K crossing above %D below 20 is a bullish signal

atr_norm.description: ATR(14) / close (the atrr_14 column); normalised average true range — higher values mean larger expected daily moves; useful for position sizing and stop calibration

bb_pct_b.description: Bollinger %B; 0.0 = price at lower band, 1.0 = at upper band, 0.5 = at midline — values near 0 or 1 signal potential mean reversion; values outside [0,1] signal breakout

bb_width.description: Bollinger bandwidth (upper - lower) / middle; low bandwidth = squeeze (volatility contraction), breakout often follows; high bandwidth = already volatile

obv_returns.description: 1-bar percentage change in On-Balance Volume; positive and rising = institutional buying pressure confirms price move; divergence from price warns of reversal

lags.description: Lagged copies of fast-moving features give XGBoost short-term sequential context without requiring recurrent architecture
